title: Microlicia sellowiana
intro: Microlicia sellowiana, also known as "erva-azul," is a plant species belonging to the Melastomataceae family, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. Bioactive constituents of Microlicia sellowiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Microlicia sellowiana include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
